name: C.V. Ananda Bose
text: C.V. Ananda Bose: Governor of West Bengal since 2023. Appointed by President Droupadi Murmu. Former IAS officer. Governors are constitutional heads of states appointed under Article 155.

name: R.N. Ravi
text: R.N. Ravi: Former Governor of Tamil Nadu (2021-2025). Former interlocutor for Naga peace talks. Governors serve at the pleasure of the President under Article 156.

name: Arif Mohammed Khan
text: Arif Mohammed Khan: Former Governor of Kerala (2019-2024). Former Union Minister. Known for championing the Shah Bano case. Governors act as Chancellor of state universities.

name: Banwarilal Purohit
text: Banwarilal Purohit: Governor of Punjab since 2021 and Administrator of Chandigarh. Former Governor of Tamil Nadu. Senior BJP leader. Governors also serve as UT administrators.

name: Ramesh Bais
text: Ramesh Bais: Governor of Maharashtra since 2023. Former Governor of Jharkhand. Senior BJP leader. 7-term Lok Sabha MP. Governors give assent to state bills under Article 200.

name: Shiv Pratap Shukla
text: Shiv Pratap Shukla: Governor of Himachal Pradesh since 2024. Former Union Minister of State for Finance. BJP Rajya Sabha MP. Governors can reserve state bills for Presidential consideration.

name: Gulab Chand Kataria
text: Gulab Chand Kataria: Governor of Assam since 2023. Former Leader of Opposition in Rajasthan Assembly. Senior BJP leader. Governors address the first session of state legislature each year.

name: Lakshman Prasad Acharya
text: Lakshman Prasad Acharya: Governor of Sikkim and Manipur since 2023. One governor can hold charge of multiple states. Former RSS leader. Appointed under Article 153 of the Constitution.

name: Haribhau Bagde
text: Haribhau Bagde: Governor of Rajasthan since 2024. Former Speaker of Maharashtra Legislative Assembly. 6-term MLA. Governors play key role during government formation.

name: Om Prakash Mathur
text: Om Prakash Mathur: Governor of Odisha since 2024. Former BJP Rajasthan leader. Under Article 163, the Governor acts on the aid and advice of the Council of Ministers.

name: Santosh Kumar Gangwar
text: Santosh Kumar Gangwar: Governor of Jharkhand since 2024. Former Union Minister of Labour. 6-term MP from Bareilly. Governors can dissolve the state assembly on the advice of the CM.

name: Rajendra Arlekar
text: Rajendra Arlekar: Governor of Bihar since 2023. Former Governor of Himachal Pradesh. Former Speaker of Goa Assembly. Governors can recommend President's Rule under Article 356.
